review_convergence_budget.stuck_review_policy: If a review thread has no receipt after review_receipt_poll_limit polls, mark thread_not_converged, archive or set cleanup_blocked, and dispatch bounded_rescue_reviewer.

review_waves.add_review_wave_reason: Required independent cold review before release readiness can be claimed.
Rebut release-evidence overclaims and check domain-specific go/no-go conditions after cold review.

unified_release_thread_table.receipt_summary: After unarchive and a convergence follow-up, the worker produced THREE_PROJECT_GOAL_READINESS_AUDIT_RECEIPT with complete_ready=false. Its earlier snapshot was stale for source dirty/install sync after the controller commit, so adoption is limited to the core findings: worker bypass avoided COS_BOOT_RECEIPT, 019f306b was not valid readiness evidence, natural heartbeat remains unverified, ADCO legacy dirty remains evidence_only_keep_open, and no domain deliverable receipt exists.

SKM_HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT was produced from isolated worktree 29b5, but self-reported thread_id was unknown. Controller corrected the real thread id, adopted the patch after validation, and archived the worker. The patch adds COS_HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT/HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT rules, template, fixture coverage, and quality-gate checks so automation enablement is no longer counted as heartbeat run evidence.

Temporary heartbeat run receipt smoke fired twice and produced COS_HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT, but the receipt used root thread 019f2354-f00c-7132-90d7-fb6c26ff2ecf instead of smoke target 019f3090-c370-7952-91a9-ce3ca910e4ee, with target_thread_verified=false. Rejected as progress evidence and adopted only as a regression fixture.

unified_release_thread_table.deliverable: strict heartbeat run receipt target verification
unified_release_thread_table.receipt_summary: Strict temporary heartbeat target emitted COS_HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT with target_thread_id 019f30a0-cf3c-77a1-8681-4609c1927c0f, target_thread_verified=true, matching title/cwd, dispatch_required=false, dispatch_outcome=not_required_user_forbid_threads, and HEARTBEAT_RUN_STRICT_SMOKE_RECEIPT. Temporary automation was deleted and the target thread archived. This proves the stricter prompt shape can produce valid run evidence, but does not replace the long-running cos natural-fire acceptance.

Read-only reviewer found that missing worker cwd/worktree cases were documented in ADCO release notes but not yet hard-blocked in COS historical audit, activation fixtures, or quality gate. The controller adopted the finding by adding thread_cwd_missing rules to SKILL.md, ACTIVATION_PROTOCOL, README, CHIEF_OF_STAFF prompt, audit_historical_threads.py, activation-contract fixtures, history fixture, and quality_gate.sh. Reviewer archived after receipt.

unified_release_thread_table.receipt_summary: Content was consistent with local evidence, but the receipt identity field was invalid. The thread is archived and rejected as completion evidence; its failure mode is adopted only as a hardening input.

unified_release_thread_table.invalid_reason: The worker read current_time 2026-07-05T13:16:01+08:00 and automation updated_at 2026-07-05T12:49:24.944+08:00, which implies next_natural_due_at_local 2026-07-05T18:49:24.944+08:00, but it still returned verdict FAIL instead of NOT_DUE and treated a user-triggered in-progress target turn as heartbeat failure.

unified_release_thread_table.receipt_summary: The receipt had the correct worker thread_id but an invalid due-window verdict. It is archived and rejected as acceptance evidence; its failure mode is adopted only as hardening input.

The heartbeat smoke proves explicit Skill invocation can fire via Codex heartbeat and produce COS_BOOT_RECEIPT, but as of 2026-07-05T13:48:18+08:00 the long-running six-hour cos automation is not due until 2026-07-05T18:49:24.944+08:00, so natural-fire completion remains unproven rather than failed.
evidence_limits: This round proves current gates/tests, local worktree evidence, target-thread readback, and a bounded heartbeat smoke in an idle target thread; it does not prove every future Codex surface or an in-progress target thread will accept a heartbeat mid-turn.

COS_WORKER_BYPASS is now encoded in the Skill, AGENTS routing snippet, role prompts, and activation fixtures. The live rescue reviewer verified the direction by not emitting COS_BOOT_RECEIPT and later returning a readiness receipt, but the receipt still blocks goal completion on natural heartbeat, ADCO legacy dirty evidence, and missing domain receipts.

HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT/COS_HEARTBEAT_RUN_RECEIPT hardens future heartbeat runs, but the current six-hour cos natural fire is still not proven until the next due window is read back from the target thread.

The new heartbeat run receipt smoke proves the stronger receipt marker can be emitted, but it also proved unverified target readback can be misreported by the model. Release evidence therefore requires validator-enforced target_thread_verified=true and target metadata readback, not self-report alone.

The strict positive smoke used an explicit target id/title/cwd in the automation prompt, so it verifies the prompt shape and receipt schema rather than independently proving the model can discover target metadata without being given it.
create_thread returned a handler error while duplicate threads materialized; duplicate 019f3036-fe20-7a00-b5eb-14ea205bad24 was archived/interrupted and rejected as evidence.

Transient invalid placeholder THREAD_DISPATCH_RECEIPT rows with thread_id=pending or dispatch_pending were emitted during continuations and are explicitly rejected as evidence; only rows with a real thread_id or a non-empty pending_worktree_id are valid dispatch evidence.
Project-main COS threads remain open by design; only completed worker/review threads should be archived.
